execution_overview.overall_summary: Both planned tasks were executed in Python inside Docker. Task1 found a positive Spearman correlation between openness and mean participation coefficient and a significant difference between high vs low openness (consistent with the focal claim). Task2, a comparable reanalysis with 90% node retention, found no significant gender difference in participation coefficient.

deviations.description: Updated Docker base image to python:3.10-slim and ensured pip/setuptools/wheel and required Python packages (pandas, numpy, scipy, networkx, python-louvain) were installed.
deviations.reason: Initial container run failed to import networkx.

deviations.description: Added robust import path adjustments for networkx (sys.path/site-packages) and corrected accidental syntax errors from earlier edits.
deviations.reason: To ensure networkx is importable during container runtime and fix introduced syntax errors.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: The planned analysis was followed: global co-occurrence entropy, per-participant graphs, MST metrics, Louvain participation, filter cpl < 10, Spearman correlation, and Welch t-test after median split.

test_statistics.other: Welch t-test (low vs high openness): t=-2.1185, p=0.0346, n_low=258, n_high=258

result_raw.result_text: Higher openness is associated with higher participation coefficient (rho=0.127, p=0.0039). A median-split t-test also showed higher participation among the high-openness group (t=-2.12, p=0.035).

conclusion.conclusion_text: The result supports the focal claim: openness is positively associated with participation coefficient, and high-openness participants show higher participation.

deviations.description: Same Docker dependency fixes as Task1 to ensure networkx and python-louvain availability.
deviations.reason: Avoid import failures within the container.

deviations.description: Added robust import path adjustments for networkx (sys.path/site-packages) and corrected earlier syntax issues.
deviations.reason: Ensure imports succeed and code runs as intended.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Followed the 90% node retention rule (removed top 10% by eigenvector centrality), computed per-participant metrics, applied filters (cpl < 10 and d_gender != '-1'), and ran Welch t-test by gender.

result_raw.result_text: No significant difference in participation coefficient between the two largest gender groups under 90% node retention (t=-1.139, p=0.256).

conclusion.conclusion_text: This comparable analysis is not directly about the focal claim; it finds no significant gender differences in participation coefficient.
